name: Japanese Beef Stir-Fry
recipeIngredient: 12 oz Beef flank steak, partially frozen
1 large Red bell pepper, cored and thinly sliced (about 1 1/4 cups)
1 tbsp Chopped fresh gingerroot OR 1 t Ground ginger
12 oz Fresh Chinese pea pods OR 2 packages (6 oz) frozen and thawed
1 large Head escarole, about 1 lb., coarsely chopped (about 8 cups)
recipeInstructions:
1. Using a sharp knife held almost parallel to the cutting surface, cut partially frozen flank steak into very thin slices.
2. In a 12-inch skillet over medium-high heat, heat oil; add beef; cook 2 to 3 minutes, stirring frequently until browned.
3. Using a slotted spoon, remove meat to plate; keep warm.
4. To the drippings in the skillet, add red bell pepper, onion, ginger, garlic, and crushed red pepper. Cook about 4 minutes, stirring constantly until vegetables are crisp-tender.
5. Add pea pods, escarole, soy sauce, and brown sugar. Cook about 1 minute, stirring constantly until pea pods are heated through and escarole is wilted.
6. Stir beef into vegetables. Makes 4 servings.
